mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing present values of future equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 54% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.03 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and declining federal funds rates amplifies consumer spending power and corporate profit margins, creating a synergistic demand expansion that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression or signal accuracy falls below 52% in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates strengthening real economic output and higher capacity utilization, which boosts near-term corporate revenue forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during post-shock recovery periods or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (t-stat < 1.5) with INDPRO growth.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained labor market expansion and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year window or positive return frequency drops below 54% during payroll growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained annual inflation exceeding 2.5 percent forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation triggered instances or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in industrial production and rise in the unemployment rate confirms a broad-based economic contraction, which severely undermines corporate cash flows and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with >55% accuracy in out-of-sample tests or interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts signal strengthening consumer confidence and rising construction sector activity, which drives near-term economic momentum and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Signal accuracy drops below 50% during early-cycle expansion phases or 5-day forward returns show no significant predictive power (p > 0.05) for housing start growth.
